title: lophiaris lowii
intro: Lophiaris lowii, also known as the "Coralroot," is a rare, perennial orchid native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Lophiaris lowii include its potential to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects. Herbal preparations of Lophiaris lowii can be made by drying the roots and leaves, which can then be consumed as a tea or used topically in tinctures and salves.
